description: Guided, adaptive AI interview with the admin. Answers are compiled directly into the workspace graph spec (workspace, nodes, edges, agent_guardrails) — not stored as prose. Capped at 5-8 questions for the MVP; the interviewer asks follow-ups based on prior answers and can pre-fill from ingested data (org-chart CSV, HR export).
output_format: See schema/graph-spec.schema.json — the compiled output must match the workspace/nodes/edges/agent_guardrails structure.

questions.question: What is the primary product or service your company delivers?
questions.generates: Domain context injected into every agent prompt; shared vocabulary across the board

questions.question: In one sentence, what is the end-to-end objective of an operational cycle, and what is your standard delivery timeline (SLA in days)?
questions.generates: The global objective every agent optimizes toward and the master SLA

questions.question: Break your delivery process into 3-5 major phases (e.g. sourcing, assembly, quality assurance, distribution).
questions.generates: The top-level structure of the graph (clusters / swimlanes)

questions.question: Where does friction, delay, or communication breakdown historically occur in your process?
questions.generates: High-risk edges flagged for extra monitoring; priority alerting at the agents sitting on them

questions.question: Which key departments or teams are involved in the operational lifecycle?
Which outside entities (vendors, suppliers, contractors) need access to the workspace?
For each role, what capacity limits or constraints apply? (e.g. vendors juggle multiple clients; Finance needs 48 hours to approve)
questions.generates: Each node's constraint model, consumed by its agent for scheduling

questions.question: Who fills each role? (names/emails per seat, or upload an org-chart CSV / HR export)
questions.generates: Individual employee provisioning (scoped logins) — the layer that makes each person's platform their own

questions.question: What action kicks off a new operational cycle? (e.g. contract signed, inventory below threshold)
questions.generates: The entry event/edge that instantiates a workflow

questions.question: For each major action: who proposes it, who approves it, and who executes it?
When one team hands off to another, what documents or data must accompany the handoff?
questions.generates: The required-payload schema on each edge

tiers.title: AI agency & guardrails: defining the agents

questions.question: For each node's agent: may it auto-approve/execute standard requests, or only draft suggestions for human approval?
How much internal data can an external partner's agent see — only its assigned deadlines, or your internal ones too?
What is the protocol when a deadline is missed? (auto-renegotiate, alert the human, halt downstream work)
What tone should agents use when communicating across teams and with external partners?
questions.generates: Shared communication style for all agent messages
